taxonomy: Ursifolium alpinum is classified as a member of the family Saxifragaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as saxifrages or stonecrops. This family is part of the larger order Saxifragales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in temperate and boreal regions. The Saxifragales order is further classified under the clade Rosids, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes roses, apples, and many other species. Ursifolium alpinum is a unique species within the Saxifragaceae family due to its specific characteristics and adaptations to its alpine habitat. Its classification is an example of how botanists use a hierarchical system to understand the relationships between different plant species.
